title: Initialization, Indeterminate Values, and Undefined Behavior · Failure Case: C23 Initialization Tools
summary: An optimized release makes an uninitialized-state bug disappear in debug and reappear with a different compiler. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem c23 initialization tools increment in defined-state initialization audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use empty initialization and reviewed C23 facilities where supported while preserving explicit invariants and fallback tests.
Distinguish undefined, unspecified, implementation-defined, locale-specific, and defined behavior and collect required documentation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict C23 Initialization Tools · Theory C23 Initialization Tools
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 7350. Produce bounded C evidence to read only fields proven initialized by state; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
